fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #1 (HIGH). Two clones can both claim the same open ticket (the write lock is per-clone). On merge the STATUS fork resolves deterministically (lower event UUID wins, `reducer/_processors.py` ~L145) and `assignee` resolves by HLC last-writer-wins, but **both agents were told CLAIMED** and proceed to duplicate work. The resolved fork is only a replay-time `logger.warning("PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED â€¦")`, surfaced by no user-facing command. There is no two-clone concurrent-claim test (audit 7.1); `test_store_concurrency.py::test_claim_storm_one_winner` is single-clone.

## Design (two independent mechanisms)
- **Ownership is the authority for "who works this ticket": the `assignee` field** (HLC last-writer-wins). Claim-loss detection is based ONLY on assignee â€” it does NOT depend on the STATUS-fork tiebreak or on any `loser_env_id` (which is unreliable on reopened tickets). A successful `claim` keeps its existing contract; the new exit-10 is a best-effort signal that fires when a merged view already shows another clone owns the ticket.
- **The STATUS-fork resolution is surfaced separately** (for observability) via a pure derived-state record read by fsck/show.

## What / files
1. **Pure derived-state fork record** â€” `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`, at the fork-resolution branch (right after the `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` warning), append a DICT (note: a dict literal with keys and values, NOT a set) to derived state:
   `state.setdefault("status_fork_resolutions", []).append({"winner_uuid": winner_uuid, "dropped_uuid": loser_uuid})`
   Pure/idempotent â€” rebuilt identically on each full replay; NO alert_store/repo_root/external I/O. (loser_env_id is intentionally NOT stored â€” it is unreliable across reopens and is not needed, since claim-loss uses `assignee`.)
2. **fsck finding** â€” `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`: in the existing reduce loop, when a reduced ticket has a non-empty `status_fork_resolutions`, `lines.append(f"STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED: {ticket_id} â€” concurrent claim/status race resolved (dropped uuid=...)")` and `issue_count += 1`. ALSO add the kind `"status_fork_resolved"` to `_STRUCTURED_KINDS` (fsck.py:32) so the JSON transform parses out `ticket_id` into the structured `--output json` object (not just `{kind, detail}`).
3. **show surfacing** â€” `show <id> --output json` renders the full reduced ticket state, so the new `status_fork_resolutions` field appears automatically; no formatter change is needed.
4. **Best-effort claim-loss signal** â€” `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` `claim_compute`, after `push.push_after_commit(tracker)`: reduce the ticket from the LOCAL store; if its resolved `assignee` is set and `!= assignee` (this claimant) â€” i.e. a competing claim that was already on the remote won the merge that `push_after_commit` performed â€” raise `ConcurrencyMismatch` (**exit 10**) with a "claim lost on cross-clone merge (now assigned to <winner>)" message. If the assignee is still this claimant (no competing merge visible), return success unchanged.
5. **docs** â€” `docs/concurrency.md`: a short note under the fork-resolution invariant (I8) documenting the derived record â†’ fsck/show and the best-effort assignee-based exit-10.
6. **Tests** (two):
   - **(a) reducer replay-idempotency unit test** â€” `tests/unit/test_reducer_status_fork_record.py` (new): reduce an event log with two concurrent forking STATUS events; assert the reduced ticket has one `status_fork_resolutions` dict entry with the correct `winner_uuid`/`dropped_uuid`; reduce the same log again and assert the field is byte-identical (pure/idempotent, not a growing side effect).
   - **(b) two-clone integration test** â€” `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` using `two_clones`, SEQUENTIAL ordering: clone A claims the ticket and pushes to the shared remote; clone B (ticket still open locally) then claims â€” B's `push_after_commit` fetches+merges A's winning claim, so B's post-push re-read sees `assignee == A`. Assert: (i) B's claim returns exit 10 / raises ConcurrencyMismatch (the exit-10 path EXERCISED explicitly); (ii) the converged `assignee` is A and identical across both clones after they sync; (iii) the merged ticket carries `status_fork_resolutions`, `fsck` emits `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`, and `show <id> --output json` includes the `status_fork_resolutions` field.

## Scope
In: `reducer/_processors.py`, `_commands/fsck.py` (finding + `_STRUCTURED_KINDS`), `_commands/claim.py`, `docs/concurrency.md`, the two tests. Out: changing the fork-resolution rule (lower-UUID-wins stays, I8); a bespoke text annotation for `show`; distributed locking; storing loser_env_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolved STATUS forks are recorded as DICT entries in PURE derived state by the reducer (no I/O/alert_store/repo_root), asserted by `tests/unit/test_reducer_status_fork_record.py` (entry correct AND byte-identical across two replays)
- [ ] `fsck` emits a `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED` finding and `"status_fork_resolved"` is in `_STRUCTURED_KINDS`, so `fsck --output json` carries a structured `ticket_id` for it (asserted in the two-clone test); `show <id> --output json` includes `status_fork_resolutions`
- [ ] The best-effort claim-loss path returns exit 10 when the post-push re-read shows another clone is the `assignee`, and success otherwise; the two-clone integration test EXERCISES the exit-10 path explicitly (asserts exit 10 / ConcurrencyMismatch)
- [ ] The two-clone test asserts a deterministic converged assignee identical across both clones + the fork surfaced (fsck + show)
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` documents the surfacing behaviour; `make test` green
